title: 2 Kings 1
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Kings
paragraphs: 1 After Ahab died, Moab turned against Israel.

2 Ahaziah fell through the wooden screen of his upstairs room in Samaria. He hurt himself badly. So he sent messengers and told them, "Go and ask Baal-zebub, the god of Ekron. Ask if I will get well from this fall."

3 But the angel of the LORD said to Elijah the Tishbite, "Go up and meet the messengers of the king of Samaria. Ask them, 'Is there no God in Israel? Why are you going to ask Baal-zebub, the god of Ekron?'
4 This is what the LORD says: 'You will not get up from your bed. You will die for sure.'" So Elijah left.
5 The messengers went back to the king. He asked them, "Why have you come back?"

6 They answered, "A man came up to meet us. He said, 'Go back to the king who sent you. Tell him this is what the LORD says: Is there no God in Israel? Why are you sending men to ask Baal-zebub, the god of Ekron? You will not get up from your bed. You will die for sure.'"
7 The king asked them, "What kind of man came up to meet you and said these words?"
8 They answered, "He was a man with much hair. He wore a leather belt around his waist." The king said, "It was Elijah the Tishbite."
9 Then King Ahaziah sent a captain with his fifty soldiers to Elijah. The captain went up to Elijah, who was sitting on top of a hill. He said to him, "Man of God, the king says, 'Come down!'"

10 Elijah answered the captain, "If I am a man of God, let fire come down from heaven. Let it burn up you and your fifty men." And fire came down from heaven. It burned up the captain and his fifty men.
11 So the king sent another captain with his fifty men to Elijah. The captain said to him, "Man of God, the king says, 'Come down at once!'"

12 Elijah answered again, "If I am a man of God, let fire come down from heaven. Let it burn up you and your fifty men." And the fire of God came down from heaven. It burned up the captain and his fifty men.

13 So the king sent a third captain with his fifty men. The third captain went up to Elijah. He knelt down in front of him and begged him. He said, "Man of God, please value my life and the lives of these fifty servants.
14 Look, fire has come down from heaven. It burned up the first two captains and all their men. But please value my life now."
15 Then the angel of the LORD said to Elijah, "Go down with him. Do not be afraid of him." So Elijah got up and went down with him to the king.

16 Elijah said to King Ahaziah, "This is what the LORD says: Is there really no God in Israel to ask? Is that why you sent messengers to ask Baal-zebub, the god of Ekron? Therefore you will not get up from your bed. You will die for sure."
17 So Ahaziah died, just as the LORD had said through Elijah. He had no son, so Jehoram became king after him. This was in the second year of Jehoram son of Jehoshaphat, king of Judah.
18 The other things Ahaziah did, and the great things he did, are written in the Book of the History of the Kings of Israel.

title: 2 Kings 2
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Kings
paragraphs: 1 The LORD was about to take Elijah up to heaven in a whirlwind. Elijah and Elisha were walking from Gilgal.
2 Elijah said to Elisha, "Please stay here. The LORD has sent me to Bethel." But Elisha said, "As surely as the LORD lives and as you live, I will not leave you." So they went down to Bethel.
3 The group of prophets at Bethel came out to Elisha. They said, "Do you know that the LORD will take your master from you today?" "Yes, I know," he said. "Do not talk about it."
4 Elijah said to Elisha, "Please stay here. The LORD has sent me to Jericho." But Elisha said, "As surely as the LORD lives and as you live, I will not leave you." So they went to Jericho.
5 The group of prophets at Jericho came up to Elisha. They said, "Do you know that the LORD will take your master from you today?" "Yes, I know," he said. "Do not talk about it."
6 Elijah said to Elisha, "Please stay here. The LORD has sent me to the Jordan." But Elisha said, "As surely as the LORD lives and as you live, I will not leave you." So the two of them went on.
7 Fifty men from the group of prophets followed them. They stood far away and watched Elijah and Elisha by the Jordan.
8 Elijah took his cloak and rolled it up. He struck the water with it. The water split to the right and to the left. The two of them crossed over on dry ground.
9 After they crossed over, Elijah said to Elisha, "Tell me, what can I do for you before I am taken away?" Elisha said, "Please let me have a double portion of your spirit."
10 "You have asked for a hard thing," Elijah said. "But if you see me when I am taken from you, it will be yours. If not, it will not be so."
11 They were walking and talking together. Suddenly a chariot of fire with horses of fire came between them. And Elijah went up to heaven in a whirlwind.
12 Elisha watched and cried out, "My father, my father, the chariots and horsemen of Israel!" Then he saw Elijah no more. He took hold of his own clothes and tore them in two.
13 Elisha picked up the cloak that had fallen from Elijah. He went back and stood on the bank of the Jordan.
14 He took the cloak of Elijah and struck the water. "Where now is the LORD, the God of Elijah?" he asked. When he struck the water, it split to the right and to the left. Then Elisha crossed over.
15 The group of prophets from Jericho saw this from across the river. They said, "The spirit of Elijah rests on Elisha." They came to meet him and bowed down to the ground.

16 They said to Elisha, "Look, we your servants have fifty brave men. Please let them go and search for your master. Maybe the Spirit of the LORD has taken him up and put him on a mountain or in a valley." "Do not send them," Elisha said.
17 But they kept asking him until he felt ashamed. So he said, "Send them." They sent fifty men. They searched for three days but did not find Elijah.
18 They returned to Elisha in Jericho. He said to them, "Didn't I tell you not to go?"
19 The men of the city said to Elisha, "Please listen, our lord. The city is in a good place, as you can see. But the water is bad, and the land makes nothing grow."
20 "Bring me a new bowl," Elisha said. "Put some salt in it." So they brought it to him.
21 Elisha went out to the spring. He threw the salt into it and said, "This is what the LORD says: 'I have healed this water. It will no longer cause death or stop things from growing.'"
22 The water there has been good to this day, just as Elisha said.
23 From there, Elisha went up to Bethel. As he walked up the road, a group of boys came out of the city. They made fun of him, shouting, "Go up, you baldhead! Go up, you baldhead!"
24 Elisha turned around, looked at them, and spoke a curse on them in the name of the LORD. Suddenly two female bears came out of the woods. They attacked forty-two of the boys.
25 Elisha went on to Mount Carmel. From there he returned to Samaria.

title: 2 Kings 3
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Kings
paragraphs: 1 In Jehoshaphat's eighteenth year as king of Judah, Jehoram son of Ahab became king of Israel. He ruled in Samaria for twelve years.
2 He did evil in the LORD's eyes, but not as his father and mother had done. He took down the sacred stone of Baal that his father had made.
3 Still, he held on to the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat. Jeroboam had led Israel to sin, and Jehoram did not turn away.
4 Now Mesha king of Moab was a sheep farmer. He used to give the king of Israel 100,000 lambs and the wool of 100,000 rams.
5 But after Ahab died, the king of Moab turned against the king of Israel.
6 So at that time King Jehoram left Samaria and called all Israel to fight.

7 He sent a message to Jehoshaphat king of Judah: "The king of Moab has turned against me. Will you go with me to fight Moab?" "I will go," Jehoshaphat said. "I am like you. My people are your people. My horses are your horses."
8 Then he asked, "Which way should we go?" "By the road through the Desert of Edom," Joram replied.
9 So the kings of Israel, Judah, and Edom set out. They traveled a long way for seven days. They had no water for the army or the animals.
10 "No!" said the king of Israel. "The LORD has called these three kings to hand us over to Moab!"
11 But Jehoshaphat asked, "Is there no prophet of the LORD here? Let us ask the LORD through him." One servant of the king of Israel said, "Elisha son of Shaphat is here. He used to serve Elijah."
12 Jehoshaphat said, "The word of the LORD is with him." So the king of Israel, Jehoshaphat, and the king of Edom went down to him.

13 But Elisha said to the king of Israel, "Why come to me? Go to the prophets of your father and your mother!" "No," the king of Israel replied. "The LORD has called these three kings to hand us over to Moab."
14 Then Elisha said, "The LORD of Hosts lives, so I promise this. I serve him. If I did not respect Jehoshaphat king of Judah, I would not look at you.
15 But now, bring me a harp player." While the harp player played, the power of the LORD came on Elisha.
16 He said, "This is what the LORD says: 'Dig many ditches in this valley.'
17 The LORD says, 'You will not see wind or rain. But the valley will fill with water. You and your cattle and animals will drink.'
18 This is a small thing for the LORD. He will also hand the Moabites over to you.
19 You will attack every strong city and every important city. You will cut down every good tree. You will block every water spring. You will ruin every good field with stones."
20 The next morning, at the time of the morning offering, water suddenly came from Edom and filled the land.
21 Now all the Moabites had heard that the kings had come to fight them. So all the men who could carry weapons, young and old, came and stood at the border.
22 They got up early in the morning. The sun was shining on the water. To the Moabites across the way, it looked as red as blood.
23 "This is blood!" they shouted. "The kings have fought and killed one another. Now Moab, let us take the loot!"
24 But when the Moabites came to the camp of Israel, the Israelites rose up and attacked them. The Moabites ran away. The Israelites chased them into their land and killed them.

25 They destroyed the cities. Each man threw stones on every good field until it was covered. They blocked every water spring. They cut down every good tree. Only Kir-haraseth was left standing. But men with slingshots surrounded it and attacked it too.
26 When the king of Moab saw that the battle was too hard for him, he took 700 men with swords. He tried to force a way to the king of Edom, but they could not win.

27 So he took his oldest son, who would be the next king. He offered him as a burnt offering on the city wall. Great anger came against the Israelites. So they pulled back and returned to their own land.

title: 2 Kings 4
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Kings
paragraphs: 1 A wife of one of the group of prophets cried out to Elisha. "Your servant, my husband, is dead. You know he honored the LORD. Now his lender is coming to take my two children as slaves!"
2 "How can I help you?" Elisha asked. "Tell me, what do you have in the house?" She said, "Your servant has nothing but a jar of oil."
3 Elisha said, "Go and borrow empty jars from all your neighbors. Do not gather just a few.
4 Then go inside. Shut the door behind you and your sons. Pour oil into all the jars, and set the full ones aside."
5 So she left him. She shut the door behind her and her sons. They kept bringing jars to her, and she kept pouring.
6 When all the jars were full, she said to her son, "Bring me another." But he said, "There are no more jars." Then the oil stopped flowing.
7 She went and told the man of God. He said, "Go, sell the oil, and pay your debt. You and your sons can live on the rest."
8 One day Elisha went to Shunem. An important woman who lived there asked him strongly to have a meal. So whenever he passed by, he stopped there to eat.
9 The woman said to her husband, "Look, I know that this man who often comes our way is a holy man of God.
10 Please let us make a small room upstairs for him. Put in a bed, a table, a chair, and a lamp. Then he can stay there when he comes."
11 One day Elisha came to visit. He went to his upper room to lie down.
12 He said to Gehazi his servant, "Call the Shunammite woman." Gehazi called her, and she stood before him.

13 Elisha said to Gehazi, "Tell her, 'Look, you have done so much for us. What can we do for you? Can we speak for you to the king or the army commander?'" She said, "I have a home among my own people."
14 So Elisha asked, "Then what should we do for her?" Gehazi said, "Well, she has no son, and her husband is old."
15 "Call her," said Elisha. So Gehazi called her, and she stood in the doorway.
16 Elisha said, "At this time next year, you will hold a son in your arms." "No, my lord," she said. "Do not lie to your servant girl, O man of God."
17 But the woman did become pregnant. At that time the next year, she gave birth to a son, just as Elisha had told her.
18 The child grew. One day he went out to his father, who was with the workers in the field.
19 "My head! My head!" he cried to his father. So his father told a servant, "Carry him to his mother."
20 The servant picked him up and carried him to his mother. The boy sat on her lap until noon, and then he died.
21 She went up and laid him on the bed of the man of God. Then she shut the door and went out.
22 She called her husband and said, "Please send me a servant and a donkey. I must go quickly to the man of God and return."
23 "Why would you go to him today?" he asked. "It is not a New Moon or Sabbath." "Everything is all right," she said.
24 Then she put a saddle on the donkey. She told her servant, "Drive on. Do not slow down for me unless I tell you."
25 So she set out and went to the man of God at Mount Carmel. When the man of God saw her far away, he said to Gehazi his servant, "Look, there is the Shunammite woman.
26 Please run to meet her. Ask, 'Are you all right? Is your husband all right? Is your child all right?'" She answered, "Everything is all right."

27 She reached the man of God at the mountain and held on to his feet. Gehazi came to push her away. But the man of God said, "Leave her alone. She is in deep pain, and the LORD has hidden it from me."
28 Then she said, "Did I ask you for a son, my lord? Didn't I say, 'Do not lie to me'?"

29 So Elisha said to Gehazi, "Tie up your clothes for travel. Take my staff in your hand and go. If you meet anyone, do not greet him. If anyone greets you, do not answer. Then lay my staff on the boy's face."
30 The mother of the boy said, "As surely as the LORD lives and as you live, I will not leave you." So he got up and followed her.
31 Gehazi went on ahead of them. He laid the staff on the boy's face, but there was no sound or answer. So he went back to meet Elisha. He said, "The boy has not woken up."
32 When Elisha reached the house, the boy was lying dead on his bed.
33 So he went in, closed the door behind the two of them, and prayed to the LORD.
34 Then Elisha got on the bed and lay on the boy. He put mouth to mouth, eye to eye, and hand to hand. As he stretched himself over the boy, the boy's body became warm.
35 Elisha turned away and walked back and forth across the room. Then he got on the bed and stretched over the boy again. The boy sneezed seven times and opened his eyes.
36 Elisha called Gehazi and said, "Call the Shunammite woman." So Gehazi called her, and she came. Then Elisha said, "Pick up your son."
37 She came in, fell at his feet, and bowed to the ground. Then she picked up her son and went out.

38 When Elisha returned to Gilgal, there was a shortage of food in the land. The group of prophets sat at his feet. He said to his helper, "Put on the large pot. Boil some stew for the group of prophets."

39 One of them went out to the field to gather plants. He found a wild vine and gathered as many wild fruits as his clothes could hold. He came back and cut them up into the pot of stew. No one knew what they were.
40 They poured it out for the men to eat. But when they tasted the stew, they cried out, "There is death in the pot, O man of God!" They could not eat it.
41 Then Elisha said, "Bring some flour." He threw it into the pot and said, "Pour it out for the people to eat." And there was nothing harmful in the pot.
42 A man from Baal-shalishah came to the man of God. He brought a sack of twenty loaves of barley bread from the first ripe grain. "Give it to the people to eat," said Elisha.
43 But his servant asked, "How can I set twenty loaves before a hundred men?" "Give it to the people to eat," said Elisha. "For this is what the LORD says: 'They will eat and have some left over.'"
44 So the servant set it before them. They ate and had some left over, just as the word of the LORD had said.

title: 2 Kings 5
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Kings
paragraphs: 1 Naaman was the army commander of the king of Aram (Syria). His master respected him greatly. Through him the LORD had given victory to Aram. He was a brave and strong soldier. But he was a leper.
2 Once the Arameans went out in raiding bands. They took a little girl from the land of Israel. She served Naaman's wife.
3 She said to her mistress, "If only my master would go to the prophet in Samaria! He would heal him of his skin disease."
4 So Naaman went and told his master what the girl from Israel had said.
5 The king of Aram said, "Go now. I will send a letter to the king of Israel." So Naaman left. He took ten talents of silver, six thousand shekels of gold, and ten sets of clothes.
6 He carried the letter to the king of Israel. It said, "With this letter I am sending my servant Naaman. Please heal him of his skin disease."
7 When the king of Israel read the letter, he tore his clothes. He said, "Am I God, who kills and gives life? Why does this man ask me to heal a leper? See — he is looking for a fight with me!"

8 Elisha the man of God heard that the king had torn his clothes. He sent a message to the king. "Why did you tear your clothes? Please let the man come to me. Then he will know there is a prophet in Israel."
9 So Naaman came with his horses and chariots. He stood at the door of Elisha's house.
10 Elisha sent a messenger out to him. The messenger said, "Go and wash yourself seven times in the Jordan River. Your skin will be healed, and you will be clean."

11 But Naaman went away angry. He said, "I thought he would come out to me. I thought he would stand and call on the name of the LORD his God. I thought he would wave his hand over the sick place and heal my skin disease.
12 Are not the Abanah and Pharpar, the rivers of Damascus, better than all the waters of Israel? Could I not wash in them and be clean?" So he turned and went away in anger.

13 But Naaman's servants came near and spoke to him. "My father, if the prophet had told you to do some great thing, would you not have done it? How much more, then, when he says, 'Wash and be clean'?"
14 So Naaman went down. He dipped himself in the Jordan seven times, just as the man of God had said. His skin became fresh like a little child's. And he was clean.

15 Then Naaman and all his servants went back to the man of God. He stood before Elisha and said, "Now I know there is no God in all the world except in Israel. So please accept a gift from your servant."
16 But Elisha replied, "As surely as the LORD lives, whom I serve, I will not accept it." Naaman urged him to take it, but Elisha refused.

17 Naaman said, "If you will not, then please give your servant as much soil as two mules can carry. For your servant will never again make a burnt offering or sacrifice to any other god but the LORD.

18 But may the LORD forgive your servant in one thing. When my master goes into the temple of Rimmon to worship, he leans on my arm. So I must bow down in the temple of Rimmon. May the LORD forgive your servant in this matter."
19 "Go in peace," Elisha said. So Naaman left him and traveled a short distance.

20 Then Gehazi, the servant of Elisha the man of God, said, "Look, my master has let this Aramean, Naaman, go free. He did not accept what Naaman brought. As surely as the LORD lives, I will run after him and get something from him."
21 So Gehazi chased after Naaman. When Naaman saw him running, he got down from the chariot to meet him. He asked, "Is everything all right?"

22 "Everything is all right," Gehazi said. "My master has sent me. He says, 'Two young men from the sons of the prophets have just come to me from the hill country of Ephraim. Please give them a talent of silver and two sets of clothes.'"

23 Naaman said, "Please, take two talents." He urged Gehazi to accept them. He tied up two talents of silver in two bags with two sets of clothes. He gave them to two of his servants, who carried them ahead of Gehazi.
24 When Gehazi came to the hill, he took the gifts from the servants. He stored them in the house. Then he sent the men away, and they left.
25 Gehazi went in and stood before his master. Elisha asked him, "Gehazi, where have you been?" "Your servant did not go anywhere," he said.

26 But Elisha said, "Did not my spirit go with you when the man got down from his chariot to meet you? Is this the time to take money and clothes, olive trees and vineyards, sheep and oxen, male and female servants?
27 So the leprosy of Naaman will stay on you and your family forever!" And Gehazi left Elisha's presence. He was a leper — white as snow.

title: 2 Kings 6
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Kings
paragraphs: 1 The prophets' students said to Elisha, "Look, our meeting place is too small for us.
2 Please let us go to the Jordan. Each of us will get a log there. We will build a place to live." Elisha said, "Go."
3 Then one of them said, "Please come with your servants." Elisha replied, "I will come."
4 So Elisha went with them. When they came to the Jordan, they began to cut down trees.
5 As one man was cutting a tree, his iron axe head fell into the water. He cried, "Oh, my master, it was borrowed!"
6 The man of God asked, "Where did it fall?" The man showed him the place. Then Elisha cut a stick and threw it there. The iron floated up.
7 "Lift it out," Elisha said. The man reached out his hand and took it.
8 Now the king of Aram was at war with Israel. He talked with his officers and said, "My camp will be in this place."
9 Then the man of God sent word to the king of Israel. He said, "Be careful near this place. The Aramean army is going down there."
10 So the king of Israel sent men to check the place. Again and again Elisha warned the king. The king stayed safe in those places.
11 The king of Aram became very angry. He called his officers and said, "Tell me, who among us helps the king of Israel?"
12 One of his officers replied, "No one, my lord the king. Elisha, the prophet in Israel, tells the king of Israel your very words. He hears the words you speak in your bedroom."
13 So the king said, "Go and find where he is. I will send men to take him." They reported, "Elisha is in Dothan."
14 Then the king of Aram sent horses, chariots, and a great army. They came by night and surrounded the city.
15 Early in the morning, the servant of the man of God got up. He went out and saw an army with horses and chariots all around the city. He asked Elisha, "Oh, my master, what shall we do?"
16 Elisha answered, "Do not be afraid. **Those who are with us are more than those who are with them**."
17 Then Elisha prayed, "O LORD, please open his eyes so he can see." The LORD opened the young man's eyes. He saw that the hills were full of horses and chariots of fire all around Elisha.
18 The Aramean army came down against Elisha. He prayed to the LORD, "Please make these people blind." So the LORD made them blind, just as Elisha had asked.
19 Then Elisha told them, "This is not the way. This is not the city. Follow me, and I will take you to the man you want." And he led them to Samaria.
20 When they entered Samaria, Elisha said, "O LORD, open the eyes of these men so they can see." The LORD opened their eyes. They looked around and saw they were in Samaria.
21 When the king of Israel saw them, he asked Elisha, "My father, shall I kill them? Shall I kill them?"
22 Elisha replied, "Do not kill them. Would you kill men you have caught with your sword or bow? Set food and water before them. Let them eat and drink, then return to their master."
23 So the king prepared a great feast for them. After they ate and drank, he sent them away. They returned to their master. And the Aramean raiders did not come into Israel again.
24 Some time later, Ben-hadad king of Aram gathered his whole army. He marched up and surrounded Samaria.
25 There was a great hunger in Samaria. The army surrounded the city so long that a donkey's head sold for eighty pieces of silver. A small cup of dove's dung sold for five pieces of silver.
26 The king of Israel was walking on the city wall. A woman cried out to him, "Help me, my lord the king!"
27 He answered, "If the LORD does not help you, where can I find help for you? From the threshing floor or the winepress?"
28 Then the king asked her, "What is wrong?" She answered, "This woman said to me, 'Give up your son. We will eat him today. Tomorrow we will eat my son.'
29 So we boiled my son and ate him. The next day I said to her, 'Give up your son so we may eat him.' But she had hidden her son."
30 When the king heard the woman's words, he tore his clothes. As he walked on the wall, the people saw the sackcloth under his clothes next to his skin.
31 He said, "May God punish me hard, if the head of Elisha son of Shaphat stays on his shoulders today!"

32 Now Elisha was sitting in his house. The elders were sitting with him. The king sent a messenger ahead. But before he arrived, Elisha said to the elders, "Do you see how this murderer has sent a man to cut off my head? Look, when the messenger comes, shut the door. Keep him out. Can you hear his master's footsteps behind him?"
33 While Elisha was still speaking, the messenger came down to him. The king said, "This trouble is from the LORD. Why should I wait for the LORD any longer?"

title: 2 Kings 7
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Kings
paragraphs: 1 Then Elisha said, "Hear the word of the LORD! The LORD says this: 'About this time tomorrow at the gate of Samaria, a seah of fine flour will sell for a shekel. Two seahs of barley will sell for a shekel.'"

2 But the officer who held up the king answered the man of God. He said, "Look, even if the LORD opened windows in heaven, could this really happen?" Elisha replied, "You will see it with your own eyes. But you will not eat any of it."
3 Now four men with leprosy sat at the entrance of the city gate. They said to one another, "Why should we just sit here until we die?

4 If we go into the city, we will die there from the famine. If we sit here, we will also die. So let us go over to the camp of the Arameans. If they let us live, we will live. If they kill us, we will die."
5 So they got up at sunset and went to the camp of the Arameans. But when they came to the edge of the camp, no one was there.

6 For the Lord had made the Arameans hear a great noise. They heard chariots, horses, and a huge army. So they said to one another, "Look, the king of Israel has hired the kings of the Hittites and Egyptians to attack us."
7 So the Arameans had got up and run away at sunset. They left their tents, horses, and donkeys behind. The camp stood just as it was. They had run to save their lives.

8 When the lepers reached the edge of the camp, they went into a tent. They ate and drank. Then they carried off silver, gold, and clothing, and hid them. They came back, entered another tent, took things, and hid them too.

9 Then they said to one another, "We are not doing what is right. Today is a day of good news. If we stay silent until morning, we will be guilty of sin. Come now, let us go and tell the king's palace."

10 So they went and called out to the city gatekeepers. They said, "We went to the Aramean camp. No one was there — no sign of anyone. Only tied-up horses and donkeys, and the tents were just as they were."
11 The gatekeepers shouted the news, and it was reported inside the king's palace.

12 So the king got up in the night. He said to his servants, "Let me tell you what the Arameans have done. They know we are starving. So they have left the camp to hide in the field. They think, 'When they come out of the city, we will take them alive and enter the city.'"

13 But one of his servants replied, "Please send scouts with five of the horses left in the city. Their fate will be no worse than the rest of us here. All the Israelites left here are sure to die. So let us send them and find out."
14 Then the scouts took two chariots with horses. The king sent them after the Aramean army. He said, "Go and see."
15 They followed the tracks as far as the Jordan. The whole road was covered with clothing and gear the Arameans had thrown off in a hurry. So the scouts returned and told the king.
16 Then the people went out and took everything from the camp of the Arameans. So a seah of fine flour sold for a shekel. Two seahs of barley sold for a shekel. It happened just as the LORD had said.

17 Now the king had put the officer who held him up in charge of the gate. But the people crushed him under their feet in the gateway, and he died. It happened just as the man of God had said before, when the king came to him.
18 It happened just as the man of God had told the king: "About this time tomorrow at the gate of Samaria, two seahs of barley will sell for a shekel. A seah of fine flour will sell for a shekel."

19 And the officer had answered the man of God. He said, "Look, even if the LORD opened windows in heaven, could this really happen?" So Elisha had replied, "You will see it with your own eyes. But you will not eat any of it!"
20 And that is just what happened to him. The people crushed him under their feet in the gateway, and he died.

title: 2 Kings 8
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Kings
paragraphs: 1 Elisha had spoken to the woman whose son he brought back to life. He said, "Get up, you and your family. Go and live in a foreign land. The LORD has sent a famine for seven years on this land."
2 So the woman did what the man of God had said. She and her family lived in the land of the Philistines for seven years.
3 At the end of seven years, the woman came back from the land of the Philistines. She went to the king to ask for her house and her land.
4 The king was speaking with Gehazi, the servant of the man of God. He said, "Please tell me all the great things Elisha has done."

5 Gehazi was telling the king how Elisha brought a dead boy back to life. Just then the woman whose son Elisha had revived came in. She came to ask the king for her house and her land. So Gehazi said, "My lord the king, this is the woman. And this is the son Elisha brought back to life."

6 The king asked the woman about it, and she said it was true. So the king chose an officer for her. He said, "Give back everything that was hers. Also give her all the crops from her field from the day she left until now."
7 Then Elisha came to Damascus. Ben-hadad king of Aram was sick. Someone told the king, "The man of God has come here."
8 So the king said to Hazael, "Take a gift with you. Go and meet the man of God. Ask the LORD through him, 'Will I get well from this sickness?'"

9 So Hazael went to meet Elisha. He took with him forty camels carrying many good things from Damascus. He came and stood before Elisha and said, "Your son Ben-hadad king of Aram sent me. He asks, 'Will I get well from this sickness?'"
10 Elisha answered, "Go and tell him, 'You will surely get well.' But the LORD has shown me that he will die."
11 Elisha looked at Hazael for a long time, until Hazael felt uncomfortable. Then the man of God began to cry.

12 Hazael asked, "Why is my lord crying?" Elisha replied, "Because I know the evil you will do to the Israelites. You will burn their strong cities. You will kill their young men with the sword. You will kill their little children. You will cut open their pregnant women."
13 Hazael said, "But how could your servant, a worthless servant, do such a terrible thing?" Elisha answered, "The LORD has shown me that you will be king over Aram."
14 So Hazael left Elisha and went back to his master. The king asked him, "What did Elisha say to you?" Hazael replied, "He told me that you would surely get well."
15 But the next day Hazael took a thick cloth and dipped it in water. He spread it over the king's face, and Ben-hadad died. Then Hazael ruled as king in his place.
16 In the fifth year of Joram son of Ahab king of Israel, Jehoram son of Jehoshaphat became king of Judah after his father.
17 Jehoram was thirty-two years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem for eight years.
18 Jehoram lived like the kings of Israel, just as the family of Ahab had done. He married a daughter of Ahab and did evil before the LORD.
19 Yet the LORD did not want to destroy Judah, for the sake of His servant David. He had promised to keep a lamp for David and his descendants forever.
20 In the days of Jehoram, Edom turned against the rule of Judah and chose their own king.
21 So Jehoram crossed over to Zair with all his chariots. The Edomites surrounded him and his chariot leaders. He got up at night and attacked them. But his soldiers ran away to their homes.
22 So to this day Edom has stayed against the rule of Judah. Libnah also turned against Judah at the same time.
23 The rest of the acts of Jehoram, and all he did, are written in the book of the history of the Kings of Judah.
24 Jehoram died and joined his fathers. He was buried with them in the City of David. His son Ahaziah ruled as king in his place.
25 In the twelfth year of Joram son of Ahab king of Israel, Ahaziah son of Jehoram became king of Judah.
26 Ahaziah was twenty-two years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem for one year. His mother's name was Athaliah. She was the granddaughter of Omri king of Israel.
27 Ahaziah lived like the family of Ahab. He did evil before the LORD, just like the family of Ahab. For he had married into Ahab's family.
28 Then Ahaziah went with Joram son of Ahab to fight Hazael king of Aram at Ramoth-gilead. The Arameans wounded Joram.

29 So King Joram returned to Jezreel to heal from his wounds. The Arameans had wounded him at Ramah when he fought Hazael king of Aram. Then Ahaziah son of Jehoram king of Judah went down to Jezreel. He went to visit Joram son of Ahab, because Joram was wounded.

title: 2 Kings 9
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Kings
paragraphs: 1 Elisha the prophet called one of the young prophets. He said, "Tie up your robe at the waist. Take this small jar of oil. Go to Ramoth-gilead.
2 When you arrive, find Jehu son of Jehoshaphat, son of Nimshi. Take him away from his friends into a back room.
3 Then pour the oil on his head. Say, 'The LORD says: I make you king over Israel.' Then open the door and run. Do not wait!"
4 So the young prophet went to Ramoth-gilead.
5 When he arrived, the army leaders were sitting there. He said, "I have a message for you, commander." Jehu asked, "For which of us?" He replied, "For you, commander."
6 So Jehu got up and went into the house. The young prophet poured the oil on his head. He said, "The LORD, the God of Israel, says: 'I make you king over the LORD's people Israel.
7 You must destroy the family of your master Ahab. I will pay back for the deaths of My servants the prophets. I will pay back for all the LORD's servants whom Jezebel killed.
8 The whole family of Ahab will die. I will cut off every male of Ahab in Israel, both slave and free.
9 I will make Ahab's family like the families of Jeroboam son of Nebat and Baasha son of Ahijah.
10 On the land at Jezreel the dogs will eat Jezebel. No one will bury her.'" Then the young prophet opened the door and ran.
11 Jehu went out to the servants of his master. They asked, "Is everything all right? Why did this crazy man come to you?" He replied, "You know his kind and his strange talk."
12 They said, "That is a lie! Tell us now!" So Jehu answered, "He talked to me about this and that. He said, 'The LORD says: I make you king over Israel.'"
13 Quickly, each man took his coat and put it under Jehu on the bare steps. Then they blew the trumpet and shouted, "Jehu is king!"
14 So Jehu son of Jehoshaphat, son of Nimshi, made a secret plan against Joram. (Now Joram and all Israel had been defending Ramoth-gilead against Hazael king of Aram.

15 But King Joram had returned to Jezreel. He went there to heal from the wounds the Arameans gave him in the battle against Hazael their king.) So Jehu said, "If you commanders want to make me king, do not let anyone leave the city to tell Jezreel."
16 Then Jehu got into his chariot and went to Jezreel. Joram was resting there. Ahaziah king of Judah had gone down to see him.

17 The guard standing on the tower in Jezreel saw Jehu's troops coming. He called out, "I see a group of troops!" Joram commanded, "Choose a rider. Send him to meet them. Ask, 'Do you come in peace?'"

18 So a rider went out to meet Jehu. He said, "The king asks: 'Do you come in peace?'" Jehu replied, "What do you know about peace? Fall in behind me." The guard reported, "The messenger reached them, but he is not coming back."
19 So the king sent a second rider. He went to them and said, "The king asks: 'Do you come in peace?'" Jehu replied, "What do you know about peace? Fall in behind me."
20 Again the guard reported, "He reached them, but he is not coming back. The driver is driving like Jehu son of Nimshi — he drives like a crazy man!"

21 Joram shouted, "Get the chariot ready!" They got his chariot ready. Then Joram king of Israel and Ahaziah king of Judah set out. Each one rode in his own chariot. They met Jehu on the land of Naboth the Jezreelite.
22 When Joram saw Jehu, he asked, "Do you come in peace, Jehu?" He replied, "How can there be peace? Your mother Jezebel's false gods and witchcraft are everywhere."
23 Joram turned around and ran away. He shouted to Ahaziah, "Betrayal, Ahaziah!"
24 Then Jehu drew his bow and shot Joram between the shoulders. The arrow went through his heart. He fell down in his chariot.

25 Jehu said to Bidkar his officer, "Pick him up. Throw him into the field of Naboth the Jezreelite. Remember when you and I rode together behind his father Ahab? The LORD spoke this judgment against him:

26 'I saw the blood of Naboth and the blood of his sons yesterday, says the LORD. So I will pay you back on this same land, says the LORD.' Now pick him up and throw him on the land, just as the LORD said."

27 When King Ahaziah of Judah saw this, he ran up the road toward Beth-haggan. Jehu chased him, shouting, "Shoot him too!" They shot Ahaziah in his chariot on the hill of Gur, near Ibleam. He ran to Megiddo and died there.
28 Then his servants carried him by chariot to Jerusalem. They buried him with his fathers in his tomb in the City of David.
29 (In the eleventh year of Joram son of Ahab, Ahaziah had become king over Judah.)
30 When Jehu arrived in Jezreel, Jezebel heard about it. So she put on eye makeup, fixed her hair, and looked down from a window.
31 As Jehu came in the gate, she asked, "Do you come in peace, O Zimri, killer of your master?"
32 He looked up at the window and called out, "Who is on my side? Who?" Two or three palace servants looked down at him.
33 Jehu yelled, "Throw her down!" So they threw her down. Her blood sprayed on the wall and on the horses. The horses walked over her.
34 Then Jehu went in and ate and drank. He said, "Take care of this cursed woman. Bury her, for she was the daughter of a king."
35 But when they went out to bury her, they found only her skull, her feet, and the palms of her hands.
36 So they went back and told Jehu. He replied, "This is the word of the LORD, which He spoke through His servant Elijah the Tishbite: 'On the land at Jezreel the dogs will eat the flesh of Jezebel.
37 Jezebel's body will lie like waste in the field on the land at Jezreel. No one will be able to say: This is Jezebel.'"

title: 2 Kings 10
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Kings
paragraphs: 1 Now Ahab had seventy sons in Samaria. So Jehu wrote letters and sent them to Samaria. He sent them to the leaders of Jezreel, the elders, and the guardians of Ahab's sons. He said:
2 "When this letter comes, your master's sons are with you. You also have chariots, horses, a strong city, and weapons.
3 So choose the best of your master's sons. Set him on his father's throne. Then fight for your master's family."
4 But they were very afraid. They said, "Two kings could not stand against him. How can we?"

5 So the palace manager, the city ruler, the elders, and the guardians sent word to Jehu. They said, "We are your servants. We will do whatever you say. We will not make anyone king. Do what is good in your eyes."

6 Then Jehu wrote them a second letter. He said, "If you are on my side and will obey me, bring the heads of your master's sons to me. Bring them to Jezreel by this time tomorrow." Now the king's sons, seventy in all, were with the leading men of the city, who raised them.
7 When the letter came, they took the king's sons. They killed all seventy of them. They put the heads in baskets and sent them to Jehu at Jezreel.
8 When the messenger came, he told Jehu, "They have brought the heads of the king's sons." Jehu said, "Pile them in two piles at the gate until morning."
9 The next morning, Jehu went out and stood before all the people. He said, "You are not guilty. I made a secret plan against my master and killed him. But who killed all these?
10 So know this: not one word the LORD spoke against the family of Ahab will fail. The LORD has done what He promised through His servant Elijah."
11 So Jehu killed everyone left in Jezreel from the family of Ahab. He also killed all his great men, close friends, and priests. He left no one alive.
12 Then Jehu set out for Samaria. On the way, at Beth-eked of the Shepherds,
13 Jehu met some relatives of Ahaziah king of Judah. He asked, "Who are you?" They said, "We are relatives of Ahaziah. We have come to greet the sons of the king and the queen mother."
14 Then Jehu ordered, "Take them alive." So his men took them alive. Then they killed them at the well of Beth-eked—forty-two men. He spared none of them.

15 When Jehu left there, he found Jehonadab son of Rechab. Jehonadab was coming to meet him. Jehu greeted him and asked, "Is your heart true to mine, as my heart is to yours?" Jehonadab said, "It is!" Jehu said, "If it is, give me your hand." So he gave him his hand. Jehu helped him into his chariot.
16 Jehu said, "Come with me. See my strong love for the LORD!" So he let him ride in his chariot.
17 When Jehu came to Samaria, he killed everyone left there from the family of Ahab. He destroyed them all. This matched the word the LORD had spoken to Elijah.
18 Then Jehu brought all the people together. He said, "Ahab served Baal a little. But Jehu will serve him a lot.

19 Now call all the prophets of Baal to me. Call all his servants and all his priests. Make sure no one is missing. I have a great sacrifice for Baal. Anyone who is missing will not live." But Jehu was tricking them. He wanted to destroy the servants of Baal.
20 Jehu commanded, "Call a special meeting for Baal." So they announced it.
21 Then Jehu sent word through all Israel. All the servants of Baal came. Not one man failed to come. They entered the temple of Baal. It was full from end to end.
22 Jehu said to the keeper of the clothes, "Bring out robes for all the servants of Baal." So he brought out robes for them.
23 Next, Jehu and Jehonadab son of Rechab went into the temple of Baal. Jehu said to the servants of Baal, "Look around. Make sure no servants of the LORD are here with you—only servants of Baal."
24 Then they went in to offer sacrifices and burnt offerings. Now Jehu had placed eighty men outside. He warned them, "If anyone lets one of these men escape, he will pay with his own life."

25 When he had finished the burnt offering, Jehu said to the guards and officers, "Go in and kill them. Do not let anyone out." So the guards and officers killed them with the sword. They threw the bodies out. Then they went into the inner room of the temple of Baal.
26 They brought out the holy stone of the temple of Baal and burned it.
27 They also broke down the holy stone of Baal. Then they tore down the temple of Baal. They made it into a public toilet, which it still is today.
28 So Jehu wiped Baal out from Israel.
29 But he did not turn away from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat. Jeroboam had led Israel to sin by worshiping the golden calves at Bethel and Dan.

30 Still, the LORD said to Jehu, "You have done well in My sight. You have done all that was in My heart against the family of Ahab. So four generations of your sons will sit on the throne of Israel."
31 Yet Jehu was not careful to follow the law of the LORD, the God of Israel, with all his heart. He did not turn away from the sins of Jeroboam, who led Israel to sin.
32 In those days the LORD began to make Israel smaller. Hazael defeated the Israelites all through their land.
33 This was east of the Jordan, through all the land of Gilead. This included the region of Gad, Reuben, and Manasseh. It went from Aroer by the Arnon Valley through Gilead to Bashan.
34 As for the other acts of Jehu, all he did and all his might, they are written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel.
35 Then Jehu died like his fathers and was buried in Samaria. His son Jehoahaz became king in his place.
36 Jehu ruled over Israel in Samaria for twenty-eight years.

title: 2 Kings 11
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Kings
paragraphs: 1 When Athaliah, the mother of Ahaziah, saw that her son was dead, she killed all the royal family.

2 But Jehosheba, the daughter of King Joram, was Ahaziah's sister. She took Joash, the son of Ahaziah, and hid him from the king's sons who were being killed. She put him and his nurse in a bedroom to hide him from Athaliah. So he was not killed.
3 Joash stayed hidden with his nurse in the house of the LORD for six years. During that time, Athaliah ruled the land.

4 In the seventh year, Jehoiada sent for the captains of one hundred men, the Carites, and the guards. He brought them into the house of the LORD. There he made a promise with them and made them swear an oath. Then he showed them the king's son.
5 He told them, "This is what you must do. A third of you who start work on the Sabbath will guard the king's palace.
6 Another third will stand at the gate of Sur. The last third will stand at the gate behind the guards. You will take turns guarding the temple.
7 The two groups that finish work on the Sabbath will guard the house of the LORD for the king.
8 You must surround the king with weapons in your hands. Kill anyone who comes near the line of guards. Stay close to the king wherever he goes."

9 So the captains of one hundred men did everything Jehoiada the priest had ordered. Each one took his men — those starting work on the Sabbath and those finishing work — and came to Jehoiada the priest.
10 Then the priest gave the captains the spears and shields of King David. These came from the house of the LORD.
11 The guards stood with weapons in their hands. They surrounded the king by the altar and the temple. They stood from the south side to the north side of the temple.

12 Then Jehoiada brought out the king's son. He put the crown on him and gave him the book of God's law. He announced him as king. They anointed him. The people clapped their hands and shouted, "May the king live long!"
13 When Athaliah heard the noise from the guards and the people, she came out to the people in the house of the LORD.

14 She looked and saw the king standing by the pillar, as was the custom. The officers and trumpet players stood beside the king. All the people of the land were happy and blowing trumpets. Then Athaliah tore her clothes and screamed, "Treason! Treason!"

15 Jehoiada the priest ordered the captains of one hundred men in charge of the army. He said, "Bring her out between the lines of guards. Kill with the sword anyone who follows her." For the priest had said, "She must not be killed in the house of the LORD."
16 So they seized Athaliah as she reached the horses' gate at the palace. There they killed her.
17 Then Jehoiada made a promise between the LORD, the king, and the people. They would be the LORD's people. He also made a promise between the king and the people.

18 Then all the people of the land went to the temple of Baal. They tore it down. They broke the altars and idols to pieces. They killed Mattan, the priest of Baal, in front of the altars. Then Jehoiada the priest placed guards at the house of the LORD.

19 He took with him the captains of one hundred men, the Carites, the guards, and all the people of the land. They brought the king down from the house of the LORD. They entered the king's palace through the Gate of the Guards. Then Joash sat on the king's throne.
20 All the people of the land were happy. And the city was quiet, because Athaliah had been killed with the sword at the king's palace.
21 Joash was seven years old when he became king.

title: 2 Kings 12
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Kings
paragraphs: 1 In the seventh year of Jehu, Joash became king. He ruled in Jerusalem for forty years. His mother was Zibiah from Beersheba.
2 Joash did what was right before the LORD. He did this all the years that Jehoiada the priest taught him.
3 But the high places were not taken away. The people kept offering sacrifices and burning incense there.
4 Joash said to the priests, "Collect all the holy money brought into the house of the LORD.

5 This means the money from the count of the people, the money from promises to God, and the money people give by their own free will. Each priest should take it from his own area. Use it to fix any broken parts of the temple."
6 But by the twenty-third year of King Joash, the priests had not fixed the temple.
7 So King Joash called Jehoiada and the other priests. He said, "Why have you not fixed the temple?

8 From now on, do not take more money from your own people. Hand it over for the temple repairs." So the priests agreed. They would not take money from the people. They would not fix the temple themselves.

9 Then Jehoiada the priest took a chest. He made a hole in its lid. He set it beside the altar on the right side as people enter the house of the LORD. The priests who guarded the doorway put all the money there.
10 Whenever they saw much money in the chest, the king's writer and the high priest came up. They counted the money and tied it up in bags.
11 Then they gave the counted money to the men who watched over the work on the house of the LORD. These men paid the workers — the men who worked with wood,
12 and the men who worked with stone. They also bought wood and cut stone to fix the house of the LORD. They paid the other costs of the temple repairs.
13 But the money brought into the house of the LORD was not used to make silver basins, tools, bowls, or trumpets. It was not used for any gold or silver items for the house of the LORD.
14 Instead, the money was paid to the workers. With it they fixed the house of the LORD.
15 No one asked the men for a report on the money they paid the workers. They acted in an honest way.
16 The money from offerings for guilt and for sin was not brought into the house of the LORD. That money belonged to the priests.
17 At that time Hazael king of Aram went up and fought against Gath. He captured it. Then he turned to attack Jerusalem.

18 So King Joash of Judah took all the holy items. His fathers Jehoshaphat, Jehoram, and Ahaziah, the kings of Judah, had set them apart. He also took his own items set apart for God. He took all the gold from the storerooms of the house of the LORD and the royal palace. He sent it all to Hazael king of Aram. So Hazael went away from Jerusalem.
19 The rest of the acts of Joash and all his great deeds are written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah.
20 The servants of Joash rose up and made a secret plan. They killed him at Beth-millo, on the road down to Silla.
21 His servants Jozabad son of Shimeath and Jehozabad son of Shomer killed him. They buried him with his fathers in the City of David. His son Amaziah ruled as the next king.

title: 2 Kings 13
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Kings
paragraphs: 1 In the twenty-third year of King Joash of Judah, Jehoahaz son of Jehu became king of Israel. He ruled in Samaria for seventeen years.
2 He did what was evil before the LORD. He did the same sins that Jeroboam son of Nebat had led Israel to do. He did not turn away from them.
3 So the LORD was very angry with Israel. He gave them over again and again to Hazael king of Aram and to his son Ben-hadad.
4 Then Jehoahaz prayed and asked the LORD for help. The LORD listened to him. He saw the hard times that the king of Aram had brought on Israel.
5 So the LORD gave Israel a rescuer. They got free from the power of the Arameans. Then the people of Israel lived in their own homes as before.
6 But they did not turn away from the sins of the house of Jeroboam. They kept on walking in them. The Asherah pole even stayed standing in Samaria.
7 Jehoahaz had almost no army left. He had only fifty horsemen, ten chariots, and ten thousand foot soldiers. The king of Aram had destroyed the rest. He had made them like dust on the ground.
8 The rest of the acts of Jehoahaz, with all his great deeds and his power, are written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel.
9 Jehoahaz died and joined his fathers. They buried him in Samaria. His son Jehoash ruled as the next king.
10 In the thirty-seventh year of King Joash of Judah, Jehoash son of Jehoahaz became king of Israel in Samaria. He ruled sixteen years.
11 He did what was evil before the LORD. He did not turn away from any of the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat. He walked in them.
12 The rest of the acts of Jehoash, with all his great deeds and his power, and his war against Amaziah king of Judah, are written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel.
13 Jehoash died and joined his fathers. Jeroboam took his throne. They buried Jehoash in Samaria with the kings of Israel.
14 Elisha became sick with the illness that would kill him. Jehoash king of Israel came down to him. He wept over him and said, "My father, my father, the chariots and horsemen of Israel!"
15 Elisha said to him, "Take a bow and some arrows." So Jehoash took a bow and some arrows.
16 Then Elisha said to the king of Israel, "Put your hand on the bow." So the king put his hand on the bow. Elisha put his hands on the king's hands.

17 Elisha said, "Open the east window." So he opened it. Elisha said, "Shoot!" So he shot. Then Elisha said, "This is the LORD's arrow of victory, the arrow of victory over Aram! You will defeat the Arameans at Aphek and finish them."
18 Then Elisha said, "Take the arrows." So he took them. Elisha said to the king of Israel, "Hit the ground!" So he hit the ground three times and stopped.

19 But the man of God was angry with him. He said, "You should have hit the ground five or six times. Then you would have defeated Aram and finished them. But now you will defeat Aram only three times."
20 Then Elisha died, and they buried him. Now Moabite attackers used to come into the land every spring.

21 Once, some Israelites were burying a man. Suddenly they saw a group of attackers. So they threw the man's body into Elisha's tomb. When his body touched the bones of Elisha, the man came back to life. He stood up on his feet.
22 Hazael king of Aram brought hard times on Israel all the days of Jehoahaz.

23 But the LORD was kind to Israel. He had deep love for them. He turned toward them because of His promise to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. To this day, the LORD has not wanted to destroy them or send them away from Him.
24 When Hazael king of Aram died, his son Ben-hadad ruled as the next king.

25 Then Jehoash son of Jehoahaz took back the cities from Ben-hadad son of Hazael. Hazael had taken these cities in battle from his father Jehoahaz. Jehoash defeated Ben-hadad three times. So he took the cities of Israel back.

title: 2 Kings 14
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Kings
paragraphs: 1 In the second year of King Jehoash son of Jehoahaz of Israel, Amaziah son of Joash became king of Judah.
2 He was twenty-five years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem for twenty-nine years. His mother was Jehoaddan from Jerusalem.
3 He did what was right in the eyes of the LORD, but not like his father David. He did everything as his father Joash had done.
4 But he did not take away the high places. The people still offered sacrifices and burned incense there.
5 As soon as Amaziah held the kingdom firmly, he killed the servants who had murdered his father the king.

6 But he did not kill the sons of those murderers. He obeyed the Book of the Law of Moses. There the LORD commanded, "Fathers must not die for their children. Children must not die for their fathers. Each person must die for his own sin."
7 Amaziah killed 10,000 Edomites in the Valley of Salt. He took Sela in battle and named it Joktheel. That is still its name today.
8 Then Amaziah sent messengers to Jehoash king of Israel, son of Jehoahaz, son of Jehu. He said, "Come, let us meet face to face."

9 But Jehoash king of Israel sent this reply to Amaziah king of Judah: "A thorn bush in Lebanon sent a message to a cedar tree in Lebanon. It said, 'Give your daughter to my son in marriage.' Then a wild animal in Lebanon came by and stepped on the thorn bush.
10 You have beaten Edom, and your heart has become proud. Be happy about that and stay home. Why start a fight that will bring you down—you and Judah with you?"
11 But Amaziah would not listen. So Jehoash king of Israel marched out. He and King Amaziah of Judah faced each other at Beth-shemesh in Judah.
12 Israel beat Judah, and every man ran home.

13 At Beth-shemesh, Jehoash king of Israel captured Amaziah king of Judah, son of Joash, son of Ahaziah. Then Jehoash went to Jerusalem. He broke down the wall of Jerusalem from the Ephraim Gate to the Corner Gate—about 400 cubits (180 meters).
14 He took all the gold and silver and all the items in the house of the LORD. He also took the treasure rooms of the royal palace and some hostages. Then he returned to Samaria.
15 The other acts of Jehoash, his power, and his war with Amaziah king of Judah—are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel?
16 Jehoash died and joined his ancestors. They buried him in Samaria with the kings of Israel. His son Jeroboam became king in his place.
17 Amaziah son of Joash king of Judah lived fifteen years after Jehoash son of Jehoahaz king of Israel died.
18 The other acts of Amaziah—are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah?
19 Plotters planned against Amaziah in Jerusalem, and he ran to Lachish. But men chased him to Lachish and killed him there.
20 They carried his body back on horses. They buried him in Jerusalem with his fathers in the City of David.
21 Then all the people of Judah took Azariah, who was sixteen years old. They made him king in place of his father Amaziah.
22 Azariah was the one who rebuilt Elath. He brought it back to Judah after King Amaziah died and joined his fathers.
23 In the fifteenth year of Amaziah son of Joash king of Judah, Jeroboam son of Jehoash became king of Israel. He ruled in Samaria for forty-one years.
24 He did evil in the eyes of the LORD. He did not turn away from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, who had led Israel into sin.

25 This Jeroboam brought back the borders of Israel, from Lebo-hamath to the Sea of the Arabah. This matched the word of the LORD, the God of Israel. He had spoken it through His servant Jonah son of Amittai, the prophet from Gath-hepher.
26 The LORD saw that the suffering of the Israelites was very bitter. Both slave and free were hurting, and no one helped Israel.
27 The LORD had said He would not wipe out the name of Israel from under heaven. So He saved them through Jeroboam son of Jehoash.
28 The other acts of Jeroboam, his power, and his wars—he took back Damascus and Hamath for Israel from Judah—are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel?
29 Jeroboam died and joined his fathers, the kings of Israel. His son Zechariah became king in his place.

title: 2 Kings 15
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Kings
paragraphs: 1 In the twenty-seventh year of Jeroboam's rule over Israel, Azariah son of Amaziah became king of Judah.
2 He was sixteen years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem fifty-two years. His mother's name was Jecoliah; she was from Jerusalem.
3 He did what was right before the LORD, just as his father Amaziah had done.
4 But the high places were not taken away. The people still offered sacrifices and burned incense there.
5 The LORD struck the king with a skin disease until he died. So he lived in a separate house. His son Jotham took charge of the palace and ruled the people of the land.
6 As for the rest of the acts of Azariah and all he did, they are written in the Book of the Kings of Judah.
7 Azariah died and joined his fathers. They buried him near them in the City of David. His son Jotham ruled in his place.
8 In the thirty-eighth year of Azariah's rule over Judah, Zechariah son of Jeroboam became king of Israel. He ruled in Samaria six months.
9 He did evil before the LORD, as his fathers had done. He did not turn away from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat. Jeroboam had led Israel into those sins.
10 Then Shallum son of Jabesh made a secret plan against Zechariah. He struck him down and killed him in front of the people. Then Shallum ruled in his place.
11 As for the rest of the acts of Zechariah, they are written in the Book of the Kings of Israel.
12 So the LORD's word to Jehu came true: "Four generations of your sons will sit on the throne of Israel."
13 In the thirty-ninth year of Uzziah's rule over Judah, Shallum son of Jabesh became king. He ruled in Samaria one full month.
14 Then Menahem son of Gadi went up from Tirzah to Samaria. He struck down and killed Shallum son of Jabesh. Then Menahem ruled in his place.
15 As for the rest of the acts of Shallum and the secret plan he led, they are written in the Book of the Kings of Israel.

16 At that time Menahem set out from Tirzah and attacked Tiphsah. He attacked everyone in the area around it. They would not open their gates to him. So he attacked Tiphsah and tore open all the pregnant women.
17 In the thirty-ninth year of Azariah's rule over Judah, Menahem son of Gadi became king of Israel. He ruled in Samaria ten years.
18 He did evil before the LORD. Through all his rule he did not turn away from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat. Jeroboam had led Israel into those sins.
19 Then Pul king of Assyria attacked the land. Menahem gave Pul a thousand talents of silver. He did this to win his help and make his own rule strong.

20 Menahem took this money from the rich men of Israel. He took fifty shekels of silver from each man. He gave it to the king of Assyria. So the king of Assyria went back and did not stay in the land.
21 As for the rest of the acts of Menahem and all he did, they are written in the Book of the Kings of Israel.
22 Menahem died and joined his fathers. His son Pekahiah ruled in his place.
23 In the fiftieth year of Azariah's rule over Judah, Pekahiah son of Menahem became king of Israel. He ruled in Samaria two years.
24 He did evil before the LORD. He did not turn away from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat. Jeroboam had led Israel into those sins.

25 Then his officer Pekah son of Remaliah made a secret plan against him. Argob, Arieh, and fifty men of Gilead joined him. At the strong tower of the king's palace in Samaria, Pekah struck down and killed Pekahiah. Then Pekah ruled in his place.
26 As for the rest of the acts of Pekahiah and all he did, they are written in the Book of the Kings of Israel.
27 In the fifty-second year of Azariah's rule over Judah, Pekah son of Remaliah became king of Israel. He ruled in Samaria twenty years.
28 He did evil before the LORD. He did not turn away from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat. Jeroboam had led Israel into those sins.

29 In the days of Pekah king of Israel, Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria came. He captured Ijon, Abel-beth-maacah, Janoah, Kedesh, Hazor, Gilead, and Galilee. He also captured all the land of Naphtali. He took the people away as prisoners to Assyria.
30 Then Hoshea son of Elah led a secret plan against Pekah son of Remaliah. In the twentieth year of Jotham son of Uzziah, Hoshea attacked Pekah and killed him. Then Hoshea ruled in his place.
31 As for the rest of the acts of Pekah and all he did, they are written in the Book of the Kings of Israel.
32 In the second year of Pekah son of Remaliah's rule over Israel, Jotham son of Uzziah became king of Judah.
33 He was twenty-five years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem sixteen years. His mother's name was Jerusha daughter of Zadok.
34 He did what was right before the LORD, just as his father Uzziah had done.
35 But the high places were not taken away. The people still offered sacrifices and burned incense there. Jotham rebuilt the Upper Gate of the LORD's house.
36 As for the rest of the acts of Jotham and all he did, they are written in the Book of the Kings of Judah.
37 (In those days the LORD began to send Rezin king of Aram and Pekah son of Remaliah against Judah.)
38 Jotham died and joined his fathers. They buried him with them in the City of David his father. His son Ahaz ruled in his place.

title: 2 Kings 16
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Kings
paragraphs: 1 In the seventeenth year of Pekah son of Remaliah, Ahaz son of Jotham became king of Judah.
2 Ahaz was twenty years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem for sixteen years. Unlike David his father, he did not do what was right before the LORD his God.
3 Instead, he followed the ways of the kings of Israel. He even burned his son in the fire as a sacrifice. He copied the evil ways of the nations that the LORD had pushed out before the Israelites.
4 He also offered sacrifices and burned incense at the high places, on the hills, and under every green tree.
5 Then Rezin king of Aram and Pekah son of Remaliah king of Israel came up to fight against Jerusalem. They surrounded Ahaz but could not defeat him.
6 At that time Rezin king of Aram took Elath back for Aram. He drove out the men of Judah. He sent the Edomites into Elath, and they live there to this day.
7 So Ahaz sent messengers to Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria. He said, "I am your servant and your son. Come up and save me from the kings of Aram and Israel, who are attacking me."
8 Ahaz also took the silver and gold from the house of the LORD and from the storerooms of the king's palace. He sent it as a gift to the king of Assyria.
9 So the king of Assyria listened to him. He marched up to Damascus and captured it. He took its people to Kir as prisoners and killed Rezin.
10 Then King Ahaz went to Damascus to meet Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria. When he saw the altar in Damascus, he sent Uriah the priest a model of the altar and full plans to build it.
11 Uriah the priest built the altar by all the instructions King Ahaz had sent from Damascus. He finished it before King Ahaz returned.
12 When the king came back from Damascus, he saw the altar. He came near to it and offered sacrifices on it.
13 He offered his burnt offering and his grain offering. He poured out his drink offering. He sprinkled the blood of his peace offerings on the altar.
14 He also took the bronze altar that stood before the LORD. It was in front of the temple, between the new altar and the house of the LORD. He moved it to the north side of the new altar.

15 Then King Ahaz gave a command to Uriah the priest. He said, "Offer on the great altar the morning burnt offering and the evening grain offering. Offer the king's burnt offering and grain offering on it too. **15b** Also offer the burnt offerings, grain offerings, and drink offerings of all the people of the land. Sprinkle on this altar all the blood of the burnt offerings and sacrifices. But I will use the bronze altar to ask for guidance."
16 So Uriah the priest did just as King Ahaz had commanded.
17 King Ahaz also cut off the frames of the movable stands. He took the bronze basin from each of them. He took down the Sea from the bronze oxen that were under it. He put it on a stone base.
18 Because of the king of Assyria, he also removed the Sabbath covering they had built in the temple. He closed the royal entrance outside the house of the LORD.
19 As for the rest of the acts of Ahaz, and the things he did, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah?
20 Ahaz died and joined his ancestors. They buried him with them in the City of David. His son Hezekiah became king in his place.

title: 2 Kings 17
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Kings
paragraphs: 1 In Ahaz's twelfth year as king of Judah, Hoshea son of Elah became king of Israel. He ruled in Samaria for nine years.
2 He did evil in the LORD's sight, but not like the earlier kings of Israel.
3 Shalmaneser king of Assyria attacked him. Hoshea became his servant king and paid him money.

4 But the king of Assyria found out that Hoshea had a secret plan. Hoshea sent messengers to King So of Egypt. He also stopped paying money like before. So the king of Assyria arrested Hoshea and put him in prison.
5 Then the king of Assyria invaded the whole land. He marched up to Samaria and surrounded the city for three years.
6 In Hoshea's ninth year, the king of Assyria captured Samaria. He carried the Israelites away to Assyria. He settled them in Halah, in Gozan by the Habor River, and in the cities of the Medes.
7 All this happened because the people of Israel had sinned against the LORD their God. The LORD had brought them out of Egypt from under Pharaoh's hand. But they worshiped other gods.
8 They followed the customs of the nations that the LORD had driven out. They also followed the practices that the kings of Israel had started.
9 The Israelites secretly did wrong things against the LORD their God. They built high places in every town, from watchtower to strong city.
10 They set up sacred stones and Asherah poles on every high hill and under every green tree.
11 They burned sweet-smelling smoke at all the high places, like the nations the LORD had driven out. They did wicked things and made the LORD angry.
12 They served idols, though the LORD had told them, "Do not do this thing."

13 Yet the LORD warned Israel and Judah through all his prophets and seers. He said, "Turn from your wicked ways. Keep my commandments and laws. Obey the whole Law I gave your fathers through my servants the prophets."
14 But they would not listen. They refused to obey, just like their fathers who did not trust the LORD their God.

15 They rejected his laws and the covenant he had made with their fathers. They also rejected the rules he had given them. They followed worthless idols and became worthless themselves. They copied the nations around them, though the LORD had said not to.
16 They left behind all the commandments of the LORD their God. They made two metal calf idols and an Asherah pole. They bowed down to the stars and powers of the sky and served Baal.
17 They burned their sons and daughters in the fire as sacrifices. They practiced magic and fortune-telling. They gave themselves over to doing evil in the LORD's sight and made him angry.
18 So the LORD was very angry with Israel. He removed them from his presence. Only the tribe of Judah was left.
19 But even Judah did not keep the commandments of the LORD their God. They followed the customs Israel had started.
20 So the LORD rejected all the children of Israel. He let attackers hurt them and take their things. Finally he sent them away from his presence.
21 When the LORD tore Israel away from David's family, they made Jeroboam son of Nebat king. Jeroboam led Israel away from the LORD and caused them to commit a great sin.
22 The Israelites kept doing all the sins of Jeroboam. They did not turn away from them.
23 Finally, the LORD removed Israel from his presence, as he had said through all his servants the prophets. So Israel was carried away from their homeland into Assyria. They are still there today.

24 Then the king of Assyria brought people from Babylon, Cuthah, Avva, Hamath, and Sepharvaim. He settled them in the towns of Samaria to replace the Israelites. They took over Samaria and lived in its towns.
25 When the new settlers first lived there, they did not worship the LORD. So he sent lions among them, and the lions killed some of them.

26 So they spoke to the king of Assyria. They said, "The peoples you moved into the cities of Samaria do not know the rules of the God of the land. So he has sent lions among them, and the lions are killing them."
27 Then the king of Assyria gave this order. "Send back one of the priests you took from Samaria. Let him go and live there and teach the rules of the God of the land."
28 So one of the priests they had taken away came back. He lived in Bethel and began to teach them how to worship the LORD.
29 But the people of each nation kept making their own gods. They set them up in the shrines on the high places that the people of Samaria had built.
30 The men of Babylon made Succoth-benoth. The men of Cuth made Nergal. The men of Hamath made Ashima.
31 The Avvites made Nibhaz and Tartak. The Sepharvites burned their children in the fire to their gods Adrammelech and Anammelech.
32 So the new people worshiped the LORD. But they also chose priests of all kinds to serve at the shrines on the high places.
33 They worshiped the LORD, but they also served their own gods. They followed the customs of the nations they came from.

34 To this day they still follow their old customs. None of them truly worship the LORD. None of them obey the rules, laws, and commandments the LORD gave to the children of Jacob. The LORD named Jacob "Israel."
35 The LORD had made a covenant with the Israelites. He commanded them, "Do not worship other gods or bow down to them. Do not serve them or offer sacrifices to them.
36 Instead, worship the LORD. He brought you out of Egypt with great power and a strong arm. Bow down to him and offer sacrifices to him.
37 Always be careful to obey the rules, laws, and commandments he wrote for you. Do not worship other gods.
38 Do not forget the covenant I have made with you. Do not worship other gods.
39 Worship the LORD your God, and he will save you from all your enemies."
40 But they would not listen. They kept following their old customs.
41 So these nations worshiped the LORD, but they also served their idols. To this day their children and grandchildren keep doing what their fathers did.

title: 2 Kings 18
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Kings
paragraphs: 1 In the third year of King Hoshea son of Elah of Israel, Hezekiah son of Ahaz became king of Judah.
2 He was twenty-five years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem for twenty-nine years. His mother's name was Abi, the daughter of Zechariah.
3 He did what was right in the eyes of the LORD, just as his father David had done.

4 He removed the high places and broke the sacred stone pillars into pieces. He cut down the Asherah poles. He also broke into pieces the bronze snake called Nehushtan that Moses had made. Until that time, the Israelites had burned sweet smoke to it.
5 Hezekiah trusted in the LORD, the God of Israel. No king of Judah was like him, before him or after him.
6 He stayed faithful to the LORD and did not turn from following Him. He kept the commands that the LORD had given Moses.
7 The LORD was with Hezekiah, and he did well wherever he went. He refused to obey the king of Assyria and would not serve him.
8 He defeated the Philistines as far as Gaza and its borders. He won from small watchtower to strong walled city.
9 In the fourth year of King Hezekiah, in the seventh year of King Hoshea son of Elah of Israel, Shalmaneser king of Assyria marched against Samaria and surrounded it.
10 After three years, the Assyrians captured the city. So Samaria fell in the sixth year of Hezekiah, the ninth year of Hoshea king of Israel.
11 The king of Assyria sent the Israelites away to Assyria. He settled them in Halah, in Gozan by the Habor River, and in the cities of the Medes.
12 This happened because they did not listen to the voice of the LORD their God. They broke His covenant — all that Moses the servant of the LORD had commanded. They would not listen or obey.
13 In the fourteenth year of King Hezekiah, Sennacherib king of Assyria attacked and captured all the strong walled cities of Judah.

14 So Hezekiah king of Judah sent word to the king of Assyria at Lachish. He said, "I have done wrong. Leave me alone, and I will pay whatever you ask." The king of Assyria demanded from Hezekiah 300 talents of silver and 30 talents of gold.
15 Hezekiah gave him all the silver in the house of the LORD and in the treasure rooms of the king's palace.
16 At that time, Hezekiah took the gold off the doors and doorposts of the temple of the LORD. He had covered them himself. He gave the gold to the king of Assyria.

17 Even so, the king of Assyria sent the Tartan, the Rabsaris, and the Rabshakeh from Lachish. They came with a great army to King Hezekiah at Jerusalem. They marched up to Jerusalem and stopped by the water channel of the upper pool, on the road to the Washer's Field.
18 They called for the king. So Eliakim son of Hilkiah the palace manager, Shebnah the writer, and Joah son of Asaph the record keeper went out to them.
19 The Rabshakeh said to them, "Tell Hezekiah this is what the great king, the king of Assyria, says: Why do you feel so sure?
20 You say you have a plan and strength for war, but these are only empty words. Whom do you trust, since you have rebelled against me?
21 Look, you trust in Egypt, that broken stick. It will pierce the hand of anyone who leans on it. Pharaoh king of Egypt is like that to all who trust him.
22 Maybe you say to me, 'We trust in the LORD our God.' But Hezekiah removed His high places and altars. He told Judah and Jerusalem, 'You must worship before this altar in Jerusalem.'
23 Now make a deal with my master, the king of Assyria. I will give you two thousand horses — if you can find riders for them!
24 How can you push back even one small officer of my master's servants? You depend on Egypt for war chariots and horse riders.
25 Did I come to destroy this place without the LORD? The LORD Himself said to me, 'Go up against this land and destroy it.'"

26 Then Eliakim son of Hilkiah, with Shebnah and Joah, said to the Rabshakeh, "Please speak to your servants in the Aramaic language. We understand it. Do not speak with us in Hebrew. The people on the wall can hear."

27 But the Rabshakeh replied, "Has my master sent me to speak only to you and your master? No — also to the men sitting on the wall. They too will eat their own waste and drink their own urine with you."
28 Then the Rabshakeh stood and called out loudly in Hebrew. He said, "Hear the word of the great king, the king of Assyria!
